title: handle_move SSRF: second fetch skips validate_outbound_url
description: In inbox.rs handle_move (line ~1431), a second raw reqwest::Client fetch of old_actor_url is performed to get alsoKnownAs. This fetch: (1) does not call validate_outbound_url(), so if the actor URI somehow resolves to an internal address via DNS rebinding after the initial fetch, the second fetch will connect; (2) uses default redirect policy (follows redirects), whereas FederationClient uses Policy::none(). A compromised remote server whose actor URI initially passes SSRF checks could 302 on the second request to an internal host. The first fetch_and_upsert_actor call does validate, but only on the initial request. Additionally, the response has no body size limit -- resp.json().await will buffer an arbitrarily large response into memory. Fix: use FederationClient (or at least call validate_outbound_url and set redirect(Policy::none()) and limit response size).
